page: Amazon Web Services x8i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 16.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The CPU architecture is x86_64, incorporating an L3 cache of 480 MB, alongside an L2 cache of 2 MB and an L1d cache of 48 KB. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. The instance achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores, Passmark CPU tests, and Passmark database operations. Memory bandwidth for cached small blocks is in the top 10 percent, while uncached large block reads and writes fall into the top 25 percent. In database and key-value store workloads, the server demonstrates high efficiency, processing 171.86 million Redis SET operations per second. For specialized tasks, the CPU's Advanced Vector Extensions and Advanced Matrix Extensions support top-tier performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and prompt processing for small and medium LLM models.

The primary tradeoff of the x8i.24xlarge is the complete absence of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU accelerators, which limits its suitability for local-storage-heavy database setups or large-scale GPU-based deep learning. However, its massive DDR5 memory capacity and high-density compute resources make it highly cost-efficient for memory-bound enterprise workloads. It is well-suited for large in-memory databases, high-throughput Redis deployments, heavy multi-threaded decompression tasks, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services x8i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores running at 3.9 GHz. It features 1536.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory, yielding 16.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier performance in Geekbench, Passmark database operations, and multi-threaded decompression. It also excels in memory bandwidth and Redis SET operations, processing 171.86 million operations per second. While the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network storage, its massive memory density and advanced CPU instruction sets make it highly efficient for in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 1.5 TB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier database and multi-core compute performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized Nitro instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier database performance.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring ninety-six vCPUs and over one terabyte of high-speed DDR5 RAM for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores at 3.9 GHz
1536.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory providing 16.0 GB per core
Top-tier performance in database operations and Redis SET benchmarks
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
Baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and CPU-based inference
